Name: hardware_aware_neural_network_optimization
Title: Low-Latency Neural Network Inference via Hardware-Aware Optimization
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging hardware-aware optimization techniques, we can significantly reduce the latency of neural network inference on commodity GPUs, leading to improved real-time performance and energy efficiency.
Related Work: Recent works such as PackQViT, PruneGNN, and SNIP have demonstrated the effectiveness of hardware-aware optimization techniques for neural network inference. However, these approaches have focused primarily on specific aspects of optimization, such as quantization, pruning, or mixed-precision training. Our proposal aims to develop a comprehensive framework that integrates multiple hardware-aware optimization techniques to achieve optimal low-latency inference performance.
Abstract: This proposal presents a novel approach to low-latency neural network inference via hardware-aware optimization. By combining techniques such as quantization, pruning, and mixed-precision training, we aim to develop a comprehensive framework that optimizes neural network inference for commodity GPUs. Our approach will involve a thorough analysis of the trade-offs between accuracy, latency, and energy efficiency, as well as the development of novel algorithms and data structures to support hardware-aware optimization. We will evaluate our framework using a range of benchmarks and metrics, including latency, throughput, and energy consumption.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Quantization-aware training for low-latency inference
Experiment 2: Pruning and mixed-precision training for improved energy efficiency
Experiment 3: Hardware-aware optimization for real-time neural network inference
Experiment 4: Evaluation of our framework using benchmarks such as ImageNet and CIFAR-10
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of integrating multiple hardware-aware optimization techniques
The potential trade-offs between accuracy, latency, and energy efficiency
The need for careful evaluation and benchmarking to ensure the effectiveness of our framework
